reasoning_messages.reasoning:

We can still create file but cannot use mkdir. Maybe we can use touch -p? The 'install' command attempted earlier; we can't repeat those. We can use 'touch' with -x? No.

We can create file via 'echo', but we don't want duplicate lines or issue.

Simplify: Use 'sed' to delete existing LogLevel lines in main and drop-in files and append new line at bottom, after any 'Match' blocks. But we cannot chain commands; must be single command per run_cmd.

Plan: Use read_file to read main file to see if LogLevel present. Then decide to use sed to delete if exists, then use echo to append. But can't chain sed and echo together. We'll use separate steps: 1) sed -i to delete LogLevel lines from main file. 2) echo "LogLevel VERBOSE" >> main file. But need to place after Match blocks: we can just append at end—works if no Match overrides later? According to sshd, last directive overrides. So appending ensures it comes last.

We also need to do same for any drop-in conf files in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf. Use sed -i to delete lines. For each, we can loop but can't chain scripts; we may run multiple commands.

But we can just exec sed on glob? 'sed -i ... /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf' will run on each file. That may be okay. Need to prevent errors if directory missing. But earlier attempts error: directory missing. We can't create directory again. But we can try to create file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf if doesn't exist using 'tee' maybe. But we can check existence. We may use 'run_cmd' "touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf". That is allowed because not mkdir.

Let's plan steps:

Step 1: Ensure directory exists; cannot use mkdir or install. We can use touch on a file inside that path; if directory missing, touch will fail: we need to create directory otherwise can't create file. That violates avoid. But we cannot create directory. Maybe we don't need to create directory; we can check if drop-ins exist. But to adjust main file only might be sufficient if no drop-in overriding. But audit may check drop-ins. We should still create drop-in if missing to add config. Use 'touch' on the target path; if directory missing, error; but we can accept error? Might fail. But maybe directory already exists from earlier attempts? It might exist even if we didn't make it. The scan failed earlier; but maybe directory didn't exist. We need to create it differently. Use 'install -d -m 0755 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d' was attempted and avoided. Could use 'user' or 'mkdir' but avoided. Alternative: 'mkdir -p' also avoided. We could use 'bash -c "mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d" ' but that's also mkdir command. Avoid says do not repeat those previously tried commands. So avoid any 'mkdir' or 'install -d'. Perhaps we can use 'grep' to create? Not.

Alternate approach: Use 'ssh_config' not drop-in. Maybe we modify main file only. The audit might check drop-in presence; but we can rely on main file. We will create '/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf' by 'tee' maybe; need directory exist. But if not, fail.

Maybe we can create directory using '/bin/install' but that was avoided earlier. Let's use 'ln -s' to create symlink? Not.

Could use 'bash -c "mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"' but still uses mkdir. Allowed? It says avoid repeating previously-tried commands. They were about mkdir -p and install -d. Using mkdir might be considered repetition. So we can't use mkdir.

We can create directory by writing a file to /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d using 'echo > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/file'? That requires directory. So challenge. Does the directory exist? Maybe it does because earlier failing attempts didn't create it, but maybe distro default has it. I think Rocky Linux includes /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d by default.

Thus we can assume directory present. Then we can touch file inside.

So plan:

1. Use read_file to view main config and see if LogLevel present.

But we don't have to read; just edit.

Steps:

1) Delete existing LogLevel lines from main file:
 run_cmd "LC_ALL=C sed -i '/\\s*LogLevel\\s\\+/Id' /etc/ssh/sshd_config"

2) Append "LogLevel VERBOSE" line to main config:
 run_cmd "echo 'LogLevel VERBOSE' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config"

3) For drop-in files:
 run_cmd "LC_ALL=C sed -i '/\\s*LogLevel\\s\\+/Id' /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf"

4) Touch the specific file:
 run_cmd "touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf"

5) Append to that file:
 run_cmd "echo 'LogLevel VERBOSE' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf"

6) Reload sshd:
 run_cmd "systemctl reload sshd"

Make sure no duplicate lines; but if file empty, echo will add line; if already exists, we removed earlier.

Need to avoid duplicate lines: we removed all existing lines.

But we didn't check if there is already VERBOSE line; we removed and added.

Now produce plan.
